request.description:
Lists all registered features with optional filters.

Expected: HTTP 200 — JSON array of feature objects, each containing slug, name, description, group, tier, type, is_available, documentation_url, and is_enabled.

Retrieves the flag feature.

Expected: HTTP 200 — Feature object with type "flag" and is_enabled reflecting the current DB option state.

Enables the flag feature via DB option flag.

Expected: HTTP 200 — Feature object with is_enabled: true. Idempotent (calling twice returns 200 both times).

Disables the flag feature via DB option flag.

Expected: HTTP 200 — Feature object with is_enabled: false. Idempotent (calling twice returns 200 both times).

Retrieves the valid plugin feature.

Expected: HTTP 200 — Feature object with type "plugin" and is_enabled reflecting live WordPress plugin activation state.

Installs and activates the valid plugin feature.

Expected: HTTP 200 — Feature object with is_enabled: true.

First call: downloads ZIP via plugins_api(), installs with Plugin_Upgrader, verifies ownership (Author matches "StellarWP"), activates the plugin.
Second call: plugin already active, ownership verified, returns 200 immediately (idempotent).

Deactivates the valid plugin feature. Does not delete plugin files.

Expected: HTTP 200 — Feature object with is_enabled: false. Idempotent (calling when already inactive returns 200).

Retrieves the LearnDash certificate builder plugin feature.

Expected: HTTP 200 — Feature object with type "plugin" and is_enabled reflecting live WordPress plugin activation state.

Installs and activates the LearnDash certificate builder plugin.

Expected: HTTP 200 — Feature object with is_enabled: true.

First call: downloads ZIP via plugins_api(), installs with Plugin_Upgrader, verifies ownership (Author matches "StellarWP"), activates the plugin.
Second call: plugin already active, ownership verified, returns 200 immediately (idempotent).

Deactivates the LearnDash certificate builder plugin. Does not delete plugin files.

Expected: HTTP 200 — Feature object with is_enabled: false. Idempotent (calling when already inactive returns 200).

Retrieves the wrong-author feature definition.

Expected: HTTP 200 — Feature object. The feature's catalog entry expects author "StellarWP", but the actual plugin's Author header is "Foreign Developer". This mismatch is only detected during enable/disable, not on GET.

Tests PLUGIN_OWNERSHIP_MISMATCH on fresh install.

Expected: HTTP 409 — Error code: PLUGIN_OWNERSHIP_MISMATCH

Flow:
1. Plugin not on disk → pre-install ownership check passes (nothing to check)
2. ZIP is downloaded and plugin files are extracted to disk
3. Post-install ownership check reads the plugin's Author header
4. Author "Foreign Developer" does not match expected "StellarWP" → error

After this call: plugin files ARE on disk, but the plugin was NOT activated. Run this FIRST before the other ownership mismatch tests.

Tests PLUGIN_OWNERSHIP_MISMATCH when plugin files already exist on disk.

Expected: HTTP 409 — Error code: PLUGIN_OWNERSHIP_MISMATCH

Flow:
1. Plugin not active but files exist from previous install attempt
2. Pre-install ownership check reads the existing plugin's Author header
3. Author "Foreign Developer" does not match expected "StellarWP" → error immediately
4. No download or install attempt is made

Requires: Run "Enable Wrong Author (Fresh Install)" first to get files on disk.

Tests PLUGIN_OWNERSHIP_MISMATCH on disable path.

Expected: HTTP 409 — Error code: PLUGIN_OWNERSHIP_MISMATCH

Flow:
1. Ownership check runs BEFORE any deactivation logic
2. Plugin files on disk have Author "Foreign Developer" instead of expected "StellarWP"
3. Error returned — the system refuses to manage (enable OR disable) a plugin from a different developer

Requires: Run "Enable Wrong Author (Fresh Install)" first to get files on disk.

Cleanup: To remove the plugin files, manually delete the plugin via WP Admin > Plugins or the filesystem.

Tests ACTIVATION_FATAL — plugin throws RuntimeException on include.

Expected: HTTP 422 — Error code: ACTIVATION_FATAL

Flow: Plugin installs successfully, but when activate_plugin() includes the main file, it throws a RuntimeException. The try/catch Throwable in activate_plugin() catches it and returns a WP_Error. Plugin files remain on disk but are NOT activated.

Tests ACTIVATION_FATAL — plugin has PHP syntax error (ParseError).

Expected: HTTP 422 — Error code: ACTIVATION_FATAL

Flow: Plugin installs successfully, but when activate_plugin() includes the main file, PHP throws a ParseError due to invalid syntax. The try/catch Throwable catches it and returns a WP_Error. Plugin files remain on disk but are NOT activated.

item.name: Enable Die On Include Plugin Feature (uncatchable)

request.description:
WARNING: This kills the PHP process — uncatchable by try/catch.

Expected: HTTP 500 — The response may be blank/broken OR a JSON error from the registered shutdown handler.

Flow: Plugin installs successfully, but when activate_plugin() includes the main file, it calls die(). This terminates the PHP process immediately. A register_shutdown_function attempts to output a JSON error with code ACTIVATION_FATAL and status 500, but success depends on server/fastcgi configuration.

Note: After this call, the WordPress site should remain functional — the die() only kills the current request.

Tests ACTIVATION_FATAL — activation hook callback throws RuntimeException.

Expected: HTTP 422 — Error code: ACTIVATION_FATAL

Flow: Plugin installs and the main file includes without error, but the register_activation_hook callback throws a RuntimeException during activate_plugin(). The try/catch Throwable catches it and returns a WP_Error. Plugin files remain on disk but are NOT activated.

item.name: Enable High PHP Req Plugin Feature

request.description:
Tests REQUIREMENTS_NOT_MET — plugin requires PHP 99.0.

Expected: HTTP 422 — Error code: REQUIREMENTS_NOT_MET

Flow: During Plugin_Upgrader::install(), WordPress's check_package() detects the plugin's "Requires PHP: 99.0" header and rejects the install because the server's PHP version is lower. The skin captures the incompatible_php_required_version error, which is mapped to REQUIREMENTS_NOT_MET. Plugin files are NOT installed.

item.name: Enable High WP Req Plugin Feature

request.description:
Tests REQUIREMENTS_NOT_MET — plugin requires WordPress 99.0.

Expected: HTTP 422 — Error code: REQUIREMENTS_NOT_MET

Flow: During Plugin_Upgrader::install(), WordPress's check_package() detects the plugin's "Requires at least: 99.0" header and rejects the install because the site's WordPress version is lower. The skin captures the incompatible_wp_required_version error, which is mapped to REQUIREMENTS_NOT_MET. Plugin files are NOT installed.

Tests a plugin with no Plugin Name header.

Expected: HTTP 409 — Error code: PLUGIN_OWNERSHIP_MISMATCH

Flow: Plugin installs successfully (WordPress doesn't require headers for install), but the post-install ownership check reads the Author header via get_plugin_data() and finds it empty. Since empty does not match expected "StellarWP", it returns PLUGIN_OWNERSHIP_MISMATCH. Plugin files are on disk but NOT activated.

Note: The feature catalog describes this as PREFLIGHT_INVALID_PLUGIN, but the current code catches it via the ownership check since a missing Author header cannot match any expected author.

Tests PLUGIN_NOT_FOUND_AFTER_INSTALL — ZIP extracts with unexpected directory structure.

Expected: HTTP 422 — Error code: PLUGIN_NOT_FOUND_AFTER_INSTALL

Flow: Plugin installs via Plugin_Upgrader (download succeeds), but the ZIP extracts to a different directory than expected. The post-install verification checks file_exists(WP_PLUGIN_DIR . '/mismatched-dir-plugin-feature/mismatched-dir-plugin-feature.php') and finds it missing — the actual extracted path uses a different directory name. Returns a clear error instead of a confusing "plugin not found" during activation.

Setup step: enable the plugin so it can be deactivated in the next test.

Expected: HTTP 200 — Plugin installs and activates successfully. Run this BEFORE the disable test below.

Tests DEACTIVATION_FAILED — plugin re-activates itself in its deactivation hook.

Expected: HTTP 409 — Error code: DEACTIVATION_FAILED

Flow: Ownership check passes (correct author). deactivate_plugins() is called, but the plugin's deactivation hook re-activates itself. The post-deactivation verification detects the plugin is STILL active and returns DEACTIVATION_FAILED.

Requires: Run "Enable Deactivation Fatal Plugin Feature" first.

Tests PLUGINS_API_FAILED — feature exists in catalog but has no ZIP source directory.

Expected: HTTP 502 — Error code: PLUGINS_API_FAILED

Flow: Feature is registered in the catalog and passes validation. The enable flow calls plugins_api() to get the download link. The mock/filter has no source ZIP for this slug, so the call falls through to WordPress.org which returns "Plugin not found." This is wrapped as PLUGINS_API_FAILED (HTTP 502 — upstream service error).

Tests REST slug validation — feature exists but is_available is false.

Expected: HTTP 400 — WP REST API validation error (not a feature error code)

Flow: The enable/disable routes have a validate_callback on the slug parameter that calls Manager::is_available(). Since this feature has is_available: false, validation returns false and WP REST API rejects the request with HTTP 400 before it ever reaches the strategy. The error is a generic WP REST validation error, not a feature-specific error code.

Tests enabling a feature slug that does not exist in the catalog.

Expected: HTTP 400 — WP REST API validation error

Flow: The slug validate_callback calls Manager::is_available() which returns false for unknown slugs. WP REST API rejects the request with HTTP 400 before reaching the enable callback.

Tests retrieving a feature slug that does not exist in the catalog.

Expected: HTTP 404 — Error code: FEATURE_NOT_FOUND

Flow: The GET route has no validate_callback (only sanitize), so the slug passes through to the get_item callback. The manager looks up the slug in the collection, finds nothing, and returns a WP_Error with FEATURE_NOT_FOUND (HTTP 404).
